question_sentence: In which country did Nato members gather for their 2026 summit?
Which tech company eliminated 4,800 jobs, just over 2% of its workforce?
Which country knocked the United States out of the World Cup?
In which US city did officials order evacuations after a 37-storey skyscraper began to buckle?
What was the most popular name for baby girls in England and Wales last year, according to newly released data?
Dwindling stocks of which fish are impacting global prices, according to a new report?
Impeachment hearings began for the vice president of which Asian country?
Which pop star was added to the World Cup final half-time show?
The biggest names in fashion descended on which city for Couture Week?
China is braced for impact from a powerful typhoon given what name?
Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding celebration took place at which famous venue?
evidence: Taylor and Travis tied the knot at Madison Square Garden in New York City.

question_sentence: A Delta Airlines flight on approach to a Chicago airport was apparently struck recently by which of the following?
evidence: The pilot of Delta Flight 1076 told controllers on the night of July 4th that, “We just had a firework hit our plane,” according to air traffic control audio and the airline.

question_sentence: Team USA’s World Cup dreams came to an end this week with a 4-1 loss to which country?
evidence: Belgium defeated the US in the Round of 16. The match had been clouded by controversy following FIFA’s decision to lift USA striker Folarin Balogun’s one-game ban after a red card in the previous round.

question_sentence: According to an annual ranking of 173 cities around the world, which of the following is the “most livable” city?
evidence: Copenhagen took the No. 1 spot on the annual list from the Economist Intelligence Unit for the second year running. The cities were ranked on factors including education, stability and healthcare.

question_sentence: Why was a high-rise building in New York City that’s being converted into apartments evacuated this week?
evidence: The building in Midtown Manhattan was evacuated after structural columns buckled on the 21st floor of the 33-story building, sending bricks falling onto the street below, according to the fire department.

question_sentence: President Donald Trump joined world leaders this week for a high-stakes NATO summit in which country?
evidence: Trump traveled to Ankara, Turkey, to attend the summit.

question_sentence: The nominees for the 78th Annual Primetime Emmy Awards were unveiled this week. Which show garnered the most nominations?
evidence: HBO Max’s medical drama “The Pitt” notched 25 nominations, the most of any show. (HBO Max, like CNN, is owned by Warner Bros. Discovery)

question_sentence: Trump Accounts, a new federal savings and investment vehicle for children, went live this month. Children born between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2028, are eligible for a one-time federal pilot contribution of how much money?
evidence: Accounts must be opened by an “authorized individual” on behalf of a child. If the account opener is applying for the $1,000 seed money, that individual “must be able to claim the child as a dependent for purposes of the child tax credit.”

question_sentence: In which popular mobile game can players help recover lost Revolutionary War treasures?
evidence: Players of “June’s Journey” step into 1920s New York and search for clues to hunt down lost artifacts. The game’s creators hope that the virtual treasure hunt will lead to some real-world discoveries.

question_sentence: Which island suffered a nationwide blackout this week?
evidence: Cuba’s national electrical grid suffered a total collapse on Monday as the country faces an ongoing energy crisis, worsened by a US blockade of fuel shipments.
